commentary: Portfolio faces near-term macro headwind from inflation shock (3.8% CPI) and rising rate expectations; 'higher for longer' Fed stance pressures valuation multiples across tech/growth. However, underlying AI capex cycle and hyperscaler spending signals (+$700B combined 2026) remain intact, supporting mega-cap tech (Apple, NVIDIA, MSFT) and semiconductor suppliers (Intel, NVIDIA). S&P 500 likely to consolidate 7,300–7,500 range over 2–4 weeks pending FOMC clarity (May 28) and NVIDIA earnings (May 20). Key catalysts: NVIDIA Q1 beat (likely), FOMC hawkish hold, Apple WWDC (June 8), and summer earnings season. Macro risks: energy shock duration, FX volatility (USD strength), and China trade tension unresolved.

tickers.thesis: Apple trades near all-time highs; Intel deal provides diversification narrative but preliminary. WWDC may disappoint if Siri rollout is incremental. Q3-Q4 catalysts stronger (iPhone 17, holiday season). Neutral rating reflects valuation compression risk from higher rates vs. AI narrative tailwinds.

<cite index="32-3,32-4,32-5,32-6">Nvidia has guided for revenue of about $78 billion, plus or minus 2%, for the first quarter of fiscal 2027; going into the May 20 earnings report, investor expectations are undoubtedly high; but Nvidia's underlying demand drivers still appear strong, broad-based, and increasingly commercial; if management's long-term demand commentary proves correct, Nvidia's share price can soar even higher</cite>.

Intel stock rebounded from $18.96 (52-week low) to $130+ on Apple deal; 377% YTD rally priced in much upside. Near-term pullback risk exists (down 6% May 15), but foundry narrative remains intact. Firm customer commitments expected H2 2026; if achieved, could re-rate to $140–160 by Q4 2026. Position remains speculative; execution critical.

<cite index="17-13,17-14,17-15">Bill Ackman's Pershing Square said it built a position in MSFT stock after it pulled back and offered an attractive valuation, establishing a position at 21 times forward earnings, broadly in line with the market multiple and well below Microsoft's trading average over the last few years; Ackman called it a 'core holding'</cite>. MSFT off recent highs; summer consolidation likely before Q4 earnings refresh. Q4 2026 earnings (July 29) is next major catalyst.

Alphabet raises capex guidance but remains in valuation compression phase due to macro headwinds. Gemini integration into Google Search ongoing; near-term margins pressured by capex. Stock likely to track S&P 500 near-term; upside locked until Q2 earnings visibility improves (late July 2026).

Financials headwind from higher-for-longer rate narrative and recession risk appetite. MasterCard is defensive but not immune to consumer slowdown if inflation erodes middle-class purchasing power. FOMC May 28 may clarify rate trajectory; if Warsh signals no cuts 2026, financial stocks compress further. Hold; neutral bias reflects macro drag.
key_drivers: FOMC May 28 decision (rates held, bias hawkish)
